summary: Module division is fundamentally sound with acceptable SRP compliance, no circular dependencies, and correctly implemented SessionBackend abstraction. One medium issue: session errors misplaced in API layer. One high issue: adapter not integrated (placeholder).

issues.title: Misplaced Session Errors in API Layer
issues.description: ERROR_NOT_FOUND and ERROR_CONFLICT reference session semantics but are defined in API presentation layer
issues.recommendation: Move to session_mapping/errors.py and re-export for backward compatibility

issues.title: Incomplete Adapter Integration
issues.description: chat_completions handler returns placeholder response, adapter not connected
issues.recommendation: Connect via get_process_manager().send_message() once adapter is ready

issues.title: Parallel Protocol Abstractions
issues.description: SessionBackend and ClaudeControllerProtocol are separate abstractions serving different purposes

openai_compatible_api.responsibility: Starlette HTTP server with OpenAI-compatible endpoints

claude_node_adapter.responsibility: Protocol conversion and subprocess lifecycle management

session_mapping.responsibility: Conversation ID to session ID mapping with LRU eviction

config.responsibility: Configuration settings and OpenClaw config generator

action_items: Move ERROR_NOT_FOUND and ERROR_CONFLICT to session_mapping/errors.py
Complete adapter integration in chat_completions() handler
